title: I Store My Demons Next to the Pickled Beets
description:
Ball Jar Demon The demon lasted a good month, emitting low moans and sandpaper coughs into her ear, before Celia found an old Ball jar in the cellar, plucked the being off the cutting board with a pair of chopsticks where it had writhed since its summoning, screwed it in tightly without any empathy, and […]

The post I Store My Demons Next to the Pickled Beets appeared first on
hook: A demon summoned and promptly jarred next to the pickled beets—magical realism that treats the mundane as salvation.

title: Hacks and The Comeback Beat the Odds
description: The Comeback always seems to coinc
hook: How two shows about aging women performers in comedy beat the prestige TV odds by making ambition and reinvention matter.

title: ‘It’s More Like a Playlist’: Lavinia Spalding on Editing the Best Women’s Travel Writing
description: The editor of The Best Women's Travel Writing series on curation, grief and levity, and the stories she hopes to read more of in the future.
hook: Lavinia Spalding on why the best women's travel writing works like a playlist—assembled, personal, ungoverned by narrative rules.

title: Everyone’s a girl’s girl on TV. Until they’re not.
description: Once upon a time, women were asked if they had danced with the devil and howled at the moon. Now, a more important question is being asked: Is she a girl’s girl?  Is Amanda Batula of Bravo’s Summer House a girl’s girl? How about Tituba? Anne Boleyn? Do you think Athena, goddess of wisdom and […]
hook: When 'girl's girl' became TV's favorite test of female loyalty—tracking how a phrase weaponized friendship itself.

title: The Feeding
description: "My mom and I lived together for five years—the breast cancer years—until she died from the disease."
hook: Five years living with her mother through breast cancer. What she learned about hunger, sickness, and love in one house.
